# 3. THE MASTER TIMELINE

### 1912 — Annual Narrative Summary

***1912 Summary**

The record reflects three government land-registration transactions in 1912, each contemporaneous but legally distinct. On 1 January, a title was issued in connection with the estate, with Jonathan Zschoche later asserting a 1912 origin for T-111 [doc#279]. On 5 May, two certified Land Registration Authority documents were issued: one certifying titles T-10, T-33776, and T-91 [doc#609], and a second certifying the TCT-4497 chain derivatives T-32917, T-33415, and T-33416 [doc#610]. On 20 May, Survey Plan L-10764 subdivided Lot 2 (LRC) PSC 12602 into parcels comprising TCT-4497, issued in favor of Mary Worrick Keesey and situated in Poblacion, Mercedes [doc#572]. These three filings—title issuance, chain certification, and survey—form the documentary base of the Mary Worrick Keesey estate's registered holdings but show no evidence of contemporaneous dispute, transfer, or administrative action by named parties (Patricia Keesee Zschoche, Jonathan Zschoche, Atty. Barandon, or any ARTA respondent) in 1912.*

### 1964 — Annual Narrative Summary

*# 1964: ESTATE YEAR SUMMARY

In 1964, the Land Registration Commission certified transfer of title for land situated in Mercedes municipality, including parcels T-125, T-41, T-4110, and TCT-4497 [doc#382], establishing the foundational title chain for the contested Balane property segment. A complaint was filed [doc#436], though the document's status and complete party identification require clarification from primary source records. These concurrent events mark the documentary genesis of the property dispute track that would occupy MWK-ESTATE administration decades later, but no causal relationship between the title certification and the complaint is evident from the record.

### 1975 — Annual Narrative Summary

*# Master Case Bible — MWK-ESTATE / Mary Worrick Keesey
## Year 1975 Summary

In 1975, estate administrative activity centered on property title certification and fee processing. On 15 May, a P50 debit registration fee was processed through the Land Registration Commission [doc#104, tx#135]. On 30 May, the LRC issued a certification (Form PIA, as amended 30 September 1968) covering Lot 2-A (8,706 sq. m.) and Lot 2-D (211 sq. m.) under LCT 1, PSD-12802, with cross-references to existing titles including TCT-4497 [doc#104, status: government issued]. These transactions reflect routine estate title-chain administration undertaken during Mary Worrick Keesey's lifetime, prior to her death and the commencement of contested litigation. No civil filings, ARTA actions, or litigation activity is recorded for this year.*

### 1988 — Annual Narrative Summary

***1988: Estate Title Administration and Initial Court Filings**

In October 1988, an annotation of a Deed of Absolute Sale was recorded on title, signaling the commencement of formal estate property documentation [doc#21]. By December 1988, a court filing was executed before the Regional Trial Court, Fifth Judicial Region, Branch 64 (Camarines Norte) identifying multiple titles within the TCT-4497 chain, including T-4502, T-4503, T-4497, T-4501, and related instruments, establishing judicial notice of the contested property registers [doc#73]. These 1988 actions mark the opening of title-chain certification and court-recordation procedures that would underpin the estate's later property disputes, though no inter-party litigation or contested claim had yet materialized. The annotations and filings are administrative in character, undertaken by estate representatives to establish clear record of the Keesey holdings in the court system.*

### 1990 — Annual Narrative Summary

***1990 Summary**

In 1990, the MWK Estate commenced administrative consolidation of the TCT-4497 property chain under Mary Worrick Keesey's ownership, with a tax declaration (No. 003-0071G 025-07-003-01-039) filed on 1 January under Presidential Decree No. 464 identifying the property administrator as "Co BEK" [doc#97]. On 9 March 1990, a Deed of Sale was annotated on title, initiating the documentary trail that would later support claims in CV-26360 regarding the contested Balane succession chain [instruments_on_title#3, doc#21]. No civil litigation or ARTA administrative action is recorded for this year; the activity reflects routine estate property documentation and titling procedures.*

### 1991 — Annual Narrative Summary

*# **1991: FOUNDATIONAL ESTATE ADMINISTRATION AND POWER-OF-ATTORNEY EXECUTION**

During 1991, the MWK Estate initiated formal legal administration through execution of Special Powers of Attorney granting Geraldine K. Hoppe and Patricia K. Zschoche (appearing also as KEESEE in formal filings) authority over multiple titles in the TCT-4497 chain and related property [doc#72, doc#82]. On 26 June 1991, a government notice was issued relating to Mary Worrick Keesey's property holdings, citing titles including T-4503 [doc#371]. Concurrently, title certification and declaration activities occurred: on 17 August 1991, the Land Registration Authority issued a certified title form [doc#102], and on 20 August 1991, a Declaration of Real Property was filed under the name of Mary Worrick Keesey as administrator/owner, covering titles T-10, T-11, T-12, and T-4502 [doc#86]. On 22 August 1991, a Deed of Absolute Sale was executed by Cesar M. dela Fuente, touching the TCT-4497 property chain [doc#413]. These parallel filings—powers of attorney, title declarations, and a contemporaneous conveyance—establish the documentary foundation for estate asset identification and early transfer-authority structuring, though no causal sequence among them is established in the record.*

### 1992 — Annual Narrative Summary

***1992 SUMMARY — MWK-ESTATE**

In 1992, Patricia Keesee Zschoche (acting through attorney-in-fact Jonathan Zschoche) executed foundational estate-administration instruments: a Special Power of Attorney issued and certified by the Philippine Consulate General, Los Angeles [doc#416], executed on 1 January 1992; and a second SPA authenticated by the same consulate [doc#329] on 19 March 1992. Concurrent with these authorizations, a Deed of Absolute Sale by Cesar M. dela Fuente [doc#432] was filed on 3 March 1992 in support of civil proceedings before the RTC Camarines Norte Branch 64 [doc#441]. On 26 May 1992, tax declarations were registered with the Registry of Deeds for Camarines Norte covering properties in the TCT-4497 chain [doc#658] and estate holdings generally [doc#636]. Title certification activity followed: on 9 November 1992, LRA issued or certified a title transfer document affecting the TCT-4497 parcel set [doc#314]. A property-status inquiry from Vicento Dasit (25 May 1992) [doc#108] suggests concurrent monitoring of Philippine holdings during this period of formal title and tax documentation. The year reflects systematic estate titling and authorization work preparatory to contested-title resolution, with no evidence of ARTA administrative proceedings or LandBank litigation in 1992.*

### 1993 — Annual Narrative Summary

***1993: Title Chain Documentation and Annotations**

In 1993, the MWK-001 estate undertook systematic title-chain documentation and property transfer execution centered on the T-4497 succession. On 17 June, a formal request for transfer from T-31298 was submitted [title_transfer#9]. On 29 June, the Land Registration Authority issued an unofficial copy of judicial form No. 109 (Revised January 1991) covering multiple TCTs {T-10, T-30682, T-32478, T-34} [doc#347]. Between August and November, four successive annotations were recorded on the title instruments: a Special Power of Attorney on 20 August [instruments_on_title#4], an Affidavit of Confirmation on 11 November [instruments_on_title#5], a Deed of Absolute Sale on 26 November [instruments_on_title#6], and a second Affidavit of Confirmation also on 26 November [instruments_on_title#7]. Execution of these transfer instruments—two Affidavits of Confirmation and one Deed of Absolute Sale, all affecting T-32917—occurred on 11 November and 26 November [title_transfer#1, #2, #3]. On 26 November, Jonathan Paul Zschoche submitted an Information Request Form to the Registry of Deeds for Camarines Norte covering the consolidated TCT set {T-32917, T-33415-6, T-4497, T-47656} [doc#251]. These filings establish the documentary foundation for the contested T-4497 chain now central to CV-26360 at RTC Camarines Norte Branch 64.*

### 1994 — Annual Narrative Summary

***1994 Summary**

In 1994, the TCT-4497 chain underwent successive title annotations and transfers centered on T-32917. Jonathan Paul Zschoche initiated Registry of Deeds information requests for Camarines Norte on 11 February 1994, seeking certification records for T-32917, T-33686, T-4497, and related titles [doc#252, doc#253]. Between 11 February and 28 February, multiple deeds of absolute sale and confirmations of sale were annotated on T-32917 [instruments_on_title#8, #17–19, #25–26, doc#21], culminating in execution of a deed of absolute sale on 28 February 1994 [title_transfer#4]. A Land Registration Authority certification (JUDKLAL Form No. 109) was issued on 15 February covering T-21-6, T-32917, T-3368, and T-33686 [doc#316]. Further annotations—a petition on 30 June and an affidavit of confirmation and deed of absolute sale on 7 July—continued modifications to T-32917 [instruments_on_title#27, #20, #28, doc#21]. A separate estate administration certification was issued on 22 September 1994 for T-21 and T-33350 [doc#312]. The year reflects active title-chain management and document certification work under the TCT-4497 chain; no civil pleadings or ARTA proceedings are documented for this period.*

### 1995 — Annual Narrative Summary

*In 1995, the estate's title administration proceeded through the Land Registration Authority with certification of foundational titles (T-10, T-21, T-306, T-36668) issued 10 April [doc#319]. Beginning 17 April, a series of annotations were recorded against TCT-32917 within the contested Balane chain (T-4497 lineage), including an Affidavit of Confirmation of Sale [instruments_on_title#21, doc#21], followed on 8 September by concurrent annotations of a Deed of Donation, a Resolution, and a second Affidavit of Confirmation [instruments_on_title#22–24, doc#21]. These multiple instruments on T-32917 established the documentary record for the title chain that would later anchor Civil Case 26-360 before RTC Camarines Norte Branch 64, though no litigation was initiated in 1995 and the specific parties to these transactions remain unidentified in the provenance record.*

### 1996 — Annual Narrative Summary

***1996 Summary — MWK-ESTATE / CV-26360 / CV-6839**

In 1996, estate administration and title-chain verification work proceeded across two parallel tracks. On 13 March, two municipal resolutions from Bauan, Camarines Norte, were issued and documented as estate exhibits [doc#389, doc#577], establishing municipal-level certifications relevant to property provenance. Concurrently, title-annotation work on the TCT-4497 chain intensified: on 11 April, a request for annotation was submitted to the Registry of Deeds against T-32917 [instruments_on_title#29, title_transfer#6], and on 21 April and 21 June, special powers of attorney were annotated against T-52540 [doc#48, doc#96], indicating active management of conveyancing authority within the estate. In the parallel CV-6839 agrarian track, on 24 May, the Land Registration Authority issued an unofficial certification of title form (T-39630) [doc#42], and on 21 June, Jonathan Paul Zschoche filed an information request with the Registry of Deeds for title data on multiple agrarian titles (T-38838, T-47656, T-48335) [doc#256]. A further Registry of Deeds request by Jonathan Zschoche on 24 September sought data on T-24 and T-38838 [doc#255], confirming sustained documentary inquiry into both the TCT-4497 contested chain and the CARP title set during this period.*

### 1997 — Annual Narrative Summary

***1997: Title-Chain Documentation & Registry Engagement**

On 26 November 1997, an annotation of a Deed of Absolute Sale was recorded on TCT T-32917, a title in the contested T-4497 chain [doc#21]. The same date, Jonathan Paul Zschoche, on behalf of the MWK estate interests, submitted an Information Request Form to the Registry of Deeds for Camarines Norte, referencing titles T-32917 and T-4497 [doc#254]. These concurrent actions—annotation and registry inquiry—indicate early estate efforts to clarify and document the title chain that would later become central to CV-26360 litigation in RTC Branch 64. No civil case was filed in 1997; the administrative record reflects preliminary title verification work in preparation for subsequent estate administration.*

### 1998 — Annual Narrative Summary

***1998: Estate Administrative Year**

In 1998, the MWK-001 estate initiated parallel administrative tracks: on 15 January, Mary Worrick Keesey's heirs filed Civil Case 6839 in RTC Daet seeking determination of just compensation for agrarian titles T-3191 and T-4494 under CARP auspices, with Cesar N. dela Fuente as authorized representative [doc#352]. Concurrently, Jonathan Paul Zschoche pursued title-chain certification work on the contested Balane-line holdings (TCT-4497 family), requesting an annotation on T-32917 on 5 June [doc#21] and submitting multiple information requests to the Registry of Deeds for Camarines Norte throughout October 1998 [doc#247], generating Land Registration Authority certifications dated 1, 2, and 23 October covering the reference titles T-4497, T-48335, and related entries [doc#242, doc#15, doc#308]. These actions—the just-compensation filing (CV-6839 track) and the title-certification inquiries (TCT-4497 chain, estate-level)—represent distinct administrative work on separate title sets but shared the common goal of clarifying estate holdings and establishing clear title for distribution or defense in anticipated disputes.*

### 1999 — Annual Narrative Summary

***1999 Summary — MWK Estate & CV-26360**

Throughout 1999, Jonathan Paul Zschoche initiated systematic title-verification work with the Registry of Deeds for Camarines Norte, submitting Information Request Forms on 21 May [doc#248] and 13 August [doc#258] targeting the T-4497 chain and related certificates {T-47656, T-48335, T-49060, T-49736, T-13, T-38838, T-49061, T-50792}. Parallel annotation and certification requests were processed on the estate's behalf, including requests for annotation on T-32917 [doc#21] filed 24 February [title_transfer#7], on T-12916 [doc#265] on 29 March, and multiple judicial-form certifications issued by the Land Registration Authority in June, July, August, and September {T-14, T-51639, T-93, T-10, T-23, T-30618, T-32619, T-50, T-33, T-40718} [doc#311, doc#111, doc#412, doc#67]. On 8 December, a Notice of Hearing and Registry Receipt were filed in Civil Case 6839 [doc#351, doc#681], evidencing concurrent activity in the CARP/just-compensation track covering the agrarian titles {T-30681, T-30682, T-30683, T-4494}. The 1999 activity reflects administrative consolidation of title records and formalization of pleading service—foundational work for subsequent litigation in both the CV-26360 and CV-6839 dockets.*

### 2000 — Annual Narrative Summary

***2000 — Year Summary**

In 2000, the estate administration and CV-26360 litigation began title-chain verification work in parallel tracks. On 3 January, CV-6839 (Mary Worrick vs. DAR and LBP for just compensation on agrarian titles) was heard in RTC Daet, Branch 38, with Cesar N. dela Fuente representing the plaintiff [doc#359]. Between January and April, Jonathan Paul Zschoche submitted multiple annotation requests on T-12916 and T-32917 to the Registry of Deeds, Camarines Norte, initiating formal property-record searches [doc#265, doc#21, doc#260]. A second Registry of Deeds information request under Zschoche's name was filed on 11 November [doc#266]. The sequence reflects early efforts to clear title encumbrances on the T-4497 chain (specifically T-32917) and estate collateral (T-12916) in support of pending civil and administrative claims, though no substantive adjudication on the merits occurred during this year.*

### 2003 — Annual Narrative Summary

***2003: Title Annotation Phase**

On 23 January 2003, two sequential annotations were recorded on TCT T-32917 within the T-4497 chain: a Deed of Sale and a Confirmation of Sale [doc#21, instruments_on_title##35–36]. These annotations were administrative notations on the contested Balane-chain title and represent the first documented title modifications in the record. No pleadings, ARTA filings, or court actions are dated to 2003; the year comprises only the title-registry annotations supporting the subsequent CV-26360 (RTC Camarines Norte Branch 64) chain-of-title verification work.*

**Detailed Event Log:**

### 2004 — Annual Narrative Summary

***2004 Summary**

In August 2004, co-heirs of legal age executed a Special Power of Attorney dated 16 August 2004 to authorize representation in estate and property matters [doc#605], with a concurrent draft instrument prepared for CV-26360 litigation purposes referencing titles T-2004, T-20902, and T-4814 [doc#676]. On 17 September 2004, a bond redemption and interest voucher related to CV-6839 (the LandBank just-compensation track) was processed and verified by the processing office, bearing claim number IRO4-4281 and bearing on the T-3844 title record [doc#80]. The simultaneous execution of the power of attorney and processing of the bond voucher reflect parallel movement in estate administration and the agrarian-compensation claim, though no document indicates causal linkage between the two acts.*

### 2005 — Annual Narrative Summary

***2005 Summary — MWK-ESTATE**

In 2005, the heirs of Mary Worrick Keesey—Geraldine Keesey Hoppe, Patricia Keesee Zschoche, and Marcia Keesey—executed successive powers of attorney to manage the estate. On 1 January, they executed a Special Power of Attorney [doc#85], followed by a formally notarized version on 8 August 2005 before Vice Consul Noemi T. Diaz at the Philippine Consulate General, Los Angeles [doc#91, doc#225, doc#292, doc#430]. Between May and August, estate counsel (Ma. Carmen Agcaoili-Orena, Ida Buenaventura, and Evener J. Villasanta) coordinated via email [doc#92, doc#74, doc#98, doc#84, doc#77, doc#90, doc#83] on property administration matters relating to the contested TCT-4497 chain and other title assets [doc#281]. On 15 September, Jonathan Paul Zschoche, acting on behalf of the heirs, submitted an information request to the Registry of Deeds for Camarines Norte concerning a Deed of Absolute Sale (T-52540), which was annotated on title on the same date [doc#257, doc#48, doc#96]. On 6 October, the Municipal Treasurer of Mercedes issued a demand letter and tax declaration (T-88888) against Mary Worrick Keesey et al. [doc#94, doc#81], signaling initiation of tax compliance tracking for the estate properties. No civil litigation or ARTA proceedings were recorded in 2005; administrative coordination and title documentation dominate the year.*

### 2011 — Annual Narrative Summary

*In 2011, the estate recorded a minor financial transaction of ₱1,857 [doc#232], and on 12 December 2011, Patricia Keesee Zschoche (as plaintiff, styled "MARY WORRICK KEESEY") filed Civil Case 26-360 in the RTC, Fifth Judicial Region, Branch 38, Daet, Camarines Norte, naming the Department of Agrarian Reform and other defendants [doc#673]. The filing marked the formal initiation of the contested-title litigation that would anchor MWK-ESTATE's property recovery strategy, though no contemporaneous evidentiary record shows coordination between the estate transaction and the civil pleading.*

### 2014 — Annual Narrative Summary

***2014 — Estate Property Tax Declarations Filed**

On 23 June 2014, the estate of Mary Worrick Keesey filed a comprehensive batch of Real Property Tax Declarations under Republic Act No. 7160 across multiple jurisdictions in Camarines Norte and related provinces [docs#30, 36, 37, 105, 128, 130, 131, 134, 136, 151, 467, 468, 469]. These declarations covered properties held in the names of Mary Worrick Keesey, the heirs of Mary Worrick Keesey, and related estate interests, including parcels in Vicente (Camarines Norte), Poblacion 5 Mercedes, Adele Court Woodland Hills (Daet), and others, with OCT/TCT references including T-52537, T-30681–T-30683, T-12, and T-431 [docs#130, 134, 468]. The filings appear to constitute routine estate-level tax compliance and property documentation work, with no litigation commenced or contested claims asserted in this period. This administrative activity establishes the estate's property portfolio baseline but does not directly interconnect with contemporaneous ARTA or civil proceedings; those tracks commenced later.*

### 2015 — Annual Narrative Summary

***2015 Summary**

In 2015, the Keesey estate pursued dual administrative tracks. On 4 September, a newspaper publication notice was filed under Republic Act 9994, presumably establishing constructive notice for estate proceedings and affecting the T-24 and T-26 property set [doc#651]. Two months later, on 18 November, related filings were submitted to RTC Branch 40, Daet, Camarines Norte before Hon. Evan D. Dizon in connection with CV-6839 (the just-compensation action against LandBank), specifically addressing titles T-14, T-4494, T-4501, and T-4502 of the agrarian CARP-affected portfolio [doc#675, doc#683]. The documents confirm RTC Branch 40 as the active forum for CV-6839 matters in that period, though no substantive motion or order text is legible from the filings themselves.*

### 2016 — Annual Narrative Summary

*# 2016 Summary — MWK-ESTATE

In 2016, the Keesey heirs initiated property-transfer and title-documentation activity across the contested T-4497 chain. Between February and September, Cesar M. dela Fuente executed multiple Deeds of Absolute Sale [doc#239, doc#366, doc#415, doc#233], purporting to transfer titles within the T-52540 lineage; these deeds were notarized and verified by the Land Registration Authority [doc#415]. Concurrently, the heirs filed tax declarations (Form 1A, RA 7160) for two parcels under "HRS. OF MARY WORRICK KEESEY" on 30 March [doc#70, doc#125], accompanied by corresponding estate transactions totaling approximately ₱17,957 [tx#1, tx#2]. Title annotations on T-52540 were recorded on 10 October, including Affidavits of Loss and Special Powers of Attorney [instruments_on_title#39, #40, #49, #50, doc#48, doc#96]. On 20 October, a petition was filed with RTC Branch 41 Daet [doc#677] with a filing fee of ₱2,025 [tx#36], and an affidavit was executed by five named individuals [doc#584]. A ₱760 land-registration fee was remitted to the Land Registration Authority on 12 August [tx#38, doc#666]. **Note:** No ARTA docket activity, CV-6839 filings, or CV-26360 litigation before RTC Branch 64 is recorded in 2016; administrative and transactional work proceeded separately.*

### 2017 — Annual Narrative Summary

*# 2017 SUMMARY — MWK-ESTATE

In 2017, the estate pursued just-compensation proceedings in CV-6839 against the Department of Agrarian Reform and Land Bank of the Philippines, with hearings conducted before RTC Branch 40 (Daet, Camarines Norte) on 13 March [doc#679, doc#682] to determine compensation for agrarian-reform titles. On the same date, the estate recorded annotations on TCT-52540 reflecting issuance of a new owner's duplicate [instruments_on_title#41 doc#48, instruments_on_title#51 doc#96]; on 28 March, Cesar M. dela Fuente executed a Deed of Confirmation as registered owner [doc#240], establishing chain-of-title documentation for the disputed parcel. Estate administration concurrently collected tax declarations (Assessment GR-2014-HH-07-003-00281, filed 2 August [doc#129]) and recorded a certified affidavit from witness Elisa Antiqueña on 28 July [doc#525] concerning titles T-11, T-12, T-13, and T-14. On 21 June 2017, Cesar N. dela Fuente died [LandBank admission in CV-6839, doc#364 filed May 2018]; thereafter, all further legal actions devolved to Patricia Keesee Zschoche and Jonathan Zschoche as successors-in-interest.

---

**Note:** No 2017 ARTA administrative actions, CV-26360 pleadings, or TCT-4497 chain litigation are documented in the event list. The concurrent activity reflects title certification and tax/property administration necessary to support the CV-6839 just-compensation claim.*

### 2018 — Annual Narrative Summary

***2018 Case Summary — MWK-ESTATE**

In 2018, estate administration and title verification work proceeded in parallel tracks without material litigation advancement. On 17 May, CV-6839 (Republic v. LandBank for just compensation on the agrarian title set) was filed in RTC Branch 40, Daet [doc#364]. Contemporaneously, Jonathan Paul Zschoche submitted information requests to the Registry of Deeds for Camarines Norte on 2 August seeking verification of titles in the TCT-4497 chain (T-32917, T-52536, and related folios) [doc#261], and the Land Registration Authority issued certified copies of those title documents on the same date [doc#322, doc#607]. Estate-side property tax declarations were filed in September and October 2018 under RA 7160: one for Philippine National Bank-held property (Assessment GR-2014-HH-07-005-00220) [doc#150] and one for the Heirs of Mary Worrick Keesee (Assessment GR-2014-HH-07-001-00264) [doc#66]. These filings reflect routine estate administration—title verification and tax compliance—with no indication of settlement, settlement negotiation, or substantive motion activity in either CV-6839 or the future CV-26360 docket during this period.*

### 2019 — Annual Narrative Summary

***2019 CHRONOLOGY — MWK-ESTATE**

In 2019, the estate administration and CV-26360 litigation proceeded on separate but concurrent tracks. On 20 February, Patricia Keesee Zschoche (acting through Attorney-in-Fact Jonathan Zschoche) filed the Complaint in Civil Case 26360 at the RTC Camarines Norte Branch 64, Camarines Norte, involving titles T-52540 and T-8216 [doc#444]. Concurrently, estate property-tax documentation was processed: a Real Property Tax Declaration (Form 1A, Assessment GR-2014-HH-07-001-00267) was filed on 4 June for the Adele Court Woodland property [doc#138], and on 31 July, the Municipal Treasurer of Mercedes issued a Statement of Account for Mary Worrick Keesey's property at Vicente Basit, Daet [doc#32]. On 3 September, the Land Registration Authority issued Official Receipt 101855, reflecting a P273 debit registration fee [doc#659, tx#39], part of ongoing title-certification work. An 15 August notation regarding CV-6839 (the just-compensation track against LandBank) recorded multiple landowners/claimants (Agcaóili, Villasanta, Parungo, Oreña, Averia, De Castro) and titles T-2005, T-2019, T-4600 [doc#299], but no new substantive litigation event is documented for that year. No causal nexus between tax filings and CV-26360's pleadings is evident from the record; both progressed as routine administrative and judicial acts.*

### 2020 — Annual Narrative Summary

***2020 CASE BIBLE — MWK-ESTATE SUMMARY**

On 22 January 2020, the Land Registration Authority (LRA) issued or certified multiple Transfer Certificates of Title spanning the TCT-4497 chain and the broader estate portfolio [docs #9, 10, 11, 12, 14, 26, 33, 146, 282, 346], with the Registry of Deeds for Camarines Norte recording aggregate registration fees of ₱54,550 across ten separate transactions [txs #80, 102, 119, 125, 155, 160, 171–174]. A second batch of LRA certifications followed on 27 January [docs #10, #230], and on 29 May 2020, a demand letter was transmitted to Dionisio Estayo of Brgy. San Roque, Mercedes, Camarines Norte [doc #231]. On 24 June, CV-26360 (RTC Br. 64) generated a payment receipt from LRA reflecting ₱27,276 in additional registration fees [doc #237, tx #116]. In September–October 2020, affidavits of publication by Nicolas M. Sanchez appeared in PDBICOL, an independent Daet newspaper [docs #76, #431], and on 30 September the Registry of Deeds at Daet submitted a government document to Atty. Ma. Carmen Agcaoili of Agcaoili & Associates touching TCT-4497 and related title registers [docs #358, #79]. These transactions and certifications reflect routine estate title administration and CV-26360 docket activity; no pleading amendments, substantive rulings, or cross-track litigation developments are recorded for 2020.*

### 2021 — Annual Narrative Summary

*## 2021 CASE SUMMARY

During 2021, the MWK-ESTATE administrative track advanced title-chain certification and property documentation for the TCT-4497 contested Balane chain. On 20 May 2021, a subdivision survey and plan were prepared for Lot 2 (LRC PSD 12802, encompassing T-23, T-440, T-4497) [doc#287]. On 13 August 2021, a property tax payment receipt was issued by the Municipal Treasurer of Mercedes, Camarines Norte, reflecting a P100 debit entry [doc#236]. On 21 September 2021, a tax declaration (Assessment GR-2014-HH-07001-00274, linked to T-52540) was recorded [doc#238]. Between 18 November and 23 November 2021, the Land Registration Authority issued multiple certified true copies of title documents and verification forms referencing T-106, T-12, T-2021002, T-079-2021002127, and T-52540 [doc#20, doc#427, doc#410, doc#368], and five separate annotations were recorded on T-52540—including Special Powers of Attorney, Deeds of Confirmation, Sales (with BIR certificates), and a Partition-Subdivision Agreement [instruments_on_title#42–57, doc#48, doc#96]. These title certifications and annotations represent estate-level chain verification work undertaken in support of CV-26360 (RTC Br. 64) but do not evidence substantive motion, hearing, or settlement activity in the underlying contested-title litigation.*

### 2022 — Annual Narrative Summary

***2022 SUMMARY**

During 2022, the MWK-ESTATE administration focused on title-chain certification and registration-fee payments to the Registry of Deeds, Province of Camarines Norte. On 17 May 2022, a tax declaration (Form 1A) was filed for real property in Mercedes, Camarines Norte covering parcels T-401, T-402, T-404 [doc#152]. Beginning in late November and culminating in December 2022, the estate procured multiple Land Registration Authority title certifications spanning the contested TCT-4497 chain (including T-4497, T-32916, T-32917, T-31298, T-49060) and related parcels, with certifications issued 6–7 December 2022 and 20–28 December 2022 [doc#144, doc#46, doc#18, doc#29, doc#52, doc#107, doc#142, doc#241, doc#124, doc#45, doc#50, doc#120]. Corresponding registration fees totaling approximately ₱26,000+ were debited to the estate account with the Registry of Deeds across nine transactions on 2, 5, 6, 20, and 27 December [tx#30, tx#118, tx#146, tx#166, tx#113, tx#126, tx#148, tx#157, tx#168, tx#144, tx#115, tx#123, tx#128, tx#129, tx#130, tx#134, tx#143, tx#145, tx#150, tx#114]. A separate Land Registration Authority certification referencing the agrarian-title set (T-32914) was issued 27 December 2022 and logged to CV-6839 [doc#107], indicating title-integrity work spanning both the contested and CARP-affected parcels. No pleadings, litigation developments, or ARTA administrative actions occurred in 2022; the year reflects administrative consolidation of title records preparatory to ongoing civil proceedings.*

### 2023 — Annual Narrative Summary

***2023 CASE SUMMARY — MWK-ESTATE & CV-26360**

During 2023, the estate of Mary Worrick Keesey pursued concurrent administrative and litigation objectives centered on the TCT-4497 title chain and related property documentation. Patricia Keesee Zschoche (as heir and administrator), represented by attorney Jonathan Paul Zschoche, initiated multiple title-certification efforts through the Land Registration Authority, executing Affidavits of Loss and Adverse Claim in February 2023 [doc#284, doc#187, doc#176] and corresponding with the Provincial Assessor of Camarines Norte on 17 February and 22 February 2023 regarding estate property status [doc#165, doc#294, doc#183, doc#298]. In parallel, Patricia Keesee Zschoche filed a municipal-level affidavit in CV-26360 through attorney-in-fact Jonathan Paul Zschoche in November 2023 [doc#408], with the civil case pending before RTC Camarines Norte Branch 64. The estate also received a Court of Appeals decision and motion filing on 25 September 2023 in CA-G.R. SP No. 181607 (Torralba/Juntilla petitioners) [doc#585, doc#582], though this matter appears procedurally distinct from the primary MWK-ESTATE litigations. Extensive title-chain certification documents were requested and received from LRA throughout January–October 2023, covering titles T-4497, T-32916/32917, T-31298, and related certificates [docs#249, doc#23, doc#272, doc#96, doc#69, etc.], establishing foundational documentation for estate settlement. On 29 August 2023, correspondence was received by the Office of the Governor requesting amnesty extension for family property transfers [doc#228, doc#678]. The year reflects routine estate administration and early-stage civil pleading, with no substantive court ruling or dispositive motion yet recorded.*

**Detailed Event Log:**

The Provincial Assessor
Provincial Assessor's Office
Daet, Camarines Norte
Sir: l am writing this letter as one of the three (3) children of the late Mary Worrick
Keesey, who died in America last March 17, 1988. Per official records, our late mother left a vast tract of land in Mercedes,Cama-
rines
Norte
with an area of 139,132 sqm. covered by TCT No. T-4497 (copy is

### 2024 — Annual Narrative Summary

***2024 CASE YEAR SUMMARY — MWK-001 ESTATE**

In 2024, the estate of Mary Worrick Keesey remained in administrative consolidation and procedural holding. Patricia Keesee Zschoche, as representative, continued tax compliance filings with the Municipality of Mercedes Treasurer [doc#189, doc#196, doc#219, doc#220], generating repeated statements of account for multiple property parcels under the Keesey name throughout October [doc#193–doc#221]. Two appeals proceedings before the Court of Appeals Third Division, involving petitioners Jomil L. Torralba and Nelly Juntilla against Judge Arnel A. Dating (RTC, Daet), were advanced by reply filing in March and motion filing in May [doc#583, doc#580], though the precise connection to the Keesey estate property claims remains procedurally unclear from these documents. Estate registration work proceeded in parallel, with the Land Registration Authority (Daet office) processing official receipts and registration fees totaling P1,094 across two transactions in September and November [doc#275, doc#222, tx#103, tx#120]. No civil filings, title-chain certifications, ARTA administrative decisions, or settlement activity were recorded; CV-26360 (RTC Br. 64, mediation 2 June 2026) and CV-6839 (just-compensation vs. LandBank) remained dormant. The year reflects routine estate housekeeping absent substantive legal development.*

### 2025 — Annual Narrative Summary

***2025 Annual Summary — MWK-ESTATE / CV-26360**

During 2025, the estate pursued concurrent tracks: systematic certification of the TCT-4497 title chain through Land Registration Authority judicial-form verifications [docs#224, 270, 264, 263, 271, 274, 265, 273, 286, 317, 16, 315, 321, 606, 614, 611, 613, 309, 24, 19, 325, 324, 318, 320, 323, 310, 313, 307, 306, 591, 594]; execution of California-apostilled Special Powers of Attorney and ancillary contracts by Patricia K. Keesee Zschoche [docs#285, 280, 589]; and initiation of civil remedies. On 1 October 2025, Jonathan Zschoche filed ARTA complaint CTN SL-2025-1021-074 against Engr. Erwin H. Balane (administrator) [doc#576], followed by execution of affidavit and formal complaint filing on 7 November 2025 at RTC Camarines Norte Branch 64 in Patricia's name as represented by Jonathan as attorney-in-fact [docs#445, 421]. Concurrently, in CV-6839 (LandBank just-compensation docket), LBP Field Support Services Center corresponded with Jonathan on 11 July 2025 regarding the agrarian-title set {T-30681, T-30682, T-30683, T-4494, T-4501, T-4502, T-4503, T-14} [doc#374], and Yuzon Law Office issued confidential legal memo to Jonathan on 5 September 2025 [doc#376]. The year concluded with demand and complaint refinements directed through Barandon Law Offices [doc#598] and estate correspondence requesting transaction documentation consolidation by 27 May [doc#591]. No mediation hearing or trial disposition occurred in 2025; CV-26360 mediation remains scheduled for 2 June 2026 at RTC Br. 64, Daet.*

**Detailed Event Log:**

first request cannot be read as simple oversight—it risks being perceived as neglect of a clear 
legal duty. ### 2026 — Annual Narrative Summary

*## 2026 Case Activity Summary — MWK-ESTATE

During 2026, Patricia Keesee Zschoche (acting through her attorney-in-fact Jonathan Zschoche) and counsel Atty. Bonifacio T. Barandon Jr. prosecuted CV-26360 across multiple fronts: pleadings (complaint, answer, motions for summary judgment, and replies) were filed in the RTC Camarines Norte Branch 64 beginning 23 January 2026 [doc#422], with successive affidavits and court filings concentrated in April 2026 [docs#393–409, 462–464], culminating in a mediation deadline at RTC Daet, Branch 64, on 2 June 2026 [deadline#3]. In parallel, Jonathan Zschoche filed standalone ARTA complaints targeting administrative delays: ARTA-1210 resolved 1 January [doc#624]; ARTA-1319 and ARTA-1321 proceeded through filing and completeness certification in March–April [docs#529–530, 480]; and ARTA-0690 and ARTA-0792 received a supervisory-review petition to the Office of the Executive Secretary on 5 May 2026 [docs#457, 459]. Estate-level title certifications (TCT-4497 chain and separate agrarian holdings) were indexed in January [docs#665–666, 664], and a month-to-month lease contract was executed in February 2026 [doc#476], constituting routine property administration. The year reflects parallel procedural advance in civil litigation (CV-26360) and administrative relief (ARTA track), with no doctrinal conflict between them.*

 As we have discussed during our last meeting Article 493 of the Civil Code contains a prohibition against the co-owners from selling or alienating a definite portion of the co-owned property before it is physically partitioned among the co-owners. It does not apply here because there is no sale to speak of since it is our argument that the sale made by Cesar is void. Therefore
  - **Key Entities/Titles:** Persons: "BONIFACIO, JR. BARANDON" &lt;barandon_lawoffice@yahoo.com&gt;

# 4. MISSING / ANOMALOUS GAPS

- 🔴 **[CRITICAL] missing_primary_instrument** — 2005 Revocation of SPA (Cesar dela Fuente): Currently testimonial via Judicial Affidavit doc#441. Primary notarized instrument missing — single biggest evidence gap on the void-SPA theory.
- 🔴 **[CRITICAL] missing_primary_instrument** — Mary Worrick Keesey death certificate (PSA-issued, ~1988): Testimonial only via project memory; PSA-certified primary document not in corpus.
- 🔴 **[CRITICAL] missing_primary_instrument** — Cesar dela Fuente death certificate (2017): Referenced in LandBank's CV-6839 filing (doc#364); primary PSA certificate not yet ingested.
- 🔴 **[CRITICAL] missing_primary_instrument** — The 2016 Deed of Sale (Cesar → buyer that led to T-52540 cancellation): The void deed at issue in CV 26-360. Not directly in corpus per current scan.
